question: Is the image layout arranged with two main side-by-side panels (separated by a thin gray line) and a data table across the bottom?
Does the left panel contain a 100% stacked area chart with the title "Generation Mix (%)"?
Does the left chart's X-axis span the years 2015 to 2025 (with one tick per year) and Y-axis span 0% to 100%?
Are there exactly 6 layers in the stacked area chart arranged from bottom to top: Coal, Gas, Nuclear, Hydro, Wind, Solar?
Do the layer colors match the prompt: Coal (dark gray), Gas (orange), Nuclear (purple), Hydro (blue), Wind (teal), Solar (yellow)?
Does the Coal layer (bottom) visually decrease from ~38% in 2015 to ~18% in 2025, and the Solar layer (top) increase from ~2% to ~11%?
Do the Nuclear and Hydro layers appear to have a relatively constant thickness (approx 19% and 7% respectively) across all years?
Are there vertical dashed lines at years 2018, 2021, and 2024 with the labels "Carbon tax", "Wind expansion", and "Solar subsidy" respectively?
Does the upper section of the right panel contain a donut chart titled "Mix in 2025" with "2025" written in the center?
Does the donut chart display 6 segments with colors matching the left panel's layers?
Does the lower section of the right panel contain a line chart titled "CO2 Intensity (gCO2/kWh)"?
Is the line chart plotted as a thick red line with circle markers, showing a consistent downward trend from ~520 (2015) to ~370 (2025)?
Is the specific numerical annotation "370" present near the 2025 data point on the line chart?
Is there a compact data table at the bottom with 3 rows (labeled "Coal%", "Solar%", "CO2 intensity") and columns for years 2015–2025?
Do the values in the data table correspond to the charts (e.g., Coal% decreasing from 38 to 18, CO2 intensity decreasing from 520 to 370)?
Is the image rendered in a vector style without 3D effects or heavy shadows?
Is all text in English and clearly legible without blurring or garbled characters?
Are there any obvious visual artifacts, broken lines, or geometric distortions?
